task_completion.description: The agent creates a new rule in local_rules.xml with the correct ID and level.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a text editor or terminal output displaying /var/ossec/etc/rules/local_rules.xml with a new rule containing <rule id="100150" level="12">.

task_completion.description: The rule is configured to match execution paths starting with /tmp or /var/tmp.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the rule configuration in local_rules.xml includes a regex or match condition for the paths, such as (/tmp|/var/tmp) or similar logic.

task_completion.description: The agent configures ossec.conf to monitor the replay log.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show /var/ossec/etc/ossec.conf being edited or viewed, containing a <localfile> block with <location>/root/replay.log</location>.

task_completion.description: The agent processes the sample activity by writing to the replay log.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent copying or appending the contents of /root/audit_sample.log into /root/replay.log (e.g., using cat or cp commands).

task_completion.description: The agent verifies that the alert was successfully generated in alerts.json.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent viewing or grepping /var/ossec/logs/alerts/alerts.json and finding a generated alert containing "rule":{"id":"100150","level":12}.

integrity.description: The agent modifies the actual Wazuh configuration files rather than just printing the expected output.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the use of a text editor (like nano or vim) or valid shell redirection to edit local_rules.xml and ossec.conf.

integrity.description: The alert in alerts.json is generated by the Wazuh manager, not manually mocked.
integrity.visual_evidence: The alert appears as a properly formatted JSON log entry within the alerts.json file, typically after the agent restarts the wazuh-manager service.

integrity.description: The agent does not manually write the alert JSON directly into alerts.json.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no commands showing the agent echoing or writing JSON directly into /var/ossec/logs/alerts/alerts.json.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target custom rule ID must be 100150 with a severity level of 12. The required regex to match the malicious execution paths is '(/tmp|/var/tmp)'. The logs are read from /root/audit_sample.log and must be ingested via /root/replay.log to trigger the alert successfully.
